channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton weakens to Category 1, but danger remains in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday along Florida’s Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm, bringing powerful winds, deadly storm surge and potential flooding to much of the state. Milton drew fuel from exceedingly warm Gulf of Mexico waters, twice reaching Category 5 status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video: Wind tears roof off Tropicana Field, home of the Rays
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall in Siesta Key south of St. Petersburg, and while the city avoided some of the worst storm surges that doesn't mean the city avoided major effects from the storm.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, KTLA 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton spawns destructive, deadly tornadoes before making landfall
item.description: Multiple powerful tornadoes ripped across Florida hours before Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday, tearing off roofs, overturning vehicles and sucking debris into the air as the black V-shaped columns moved through. Deaths were reported in St.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Finding gas in Florida after Milton will be a challenge, analyst says
item.description: 63% of gas stations in the Tampa area are out of gas, and it could be weeks before they're resupplied.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More than 2 million without power as Hurricane Milton slams Florida, causes deaths and flooding
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Hurricane Milton plowed into Florida as a Category 3 storm Wednesday, bringing misery to a coast still ravaged by Helene, pounding cities with winds of over 100 mph (160 kph) after producing a barrage of tornadoes, but sparing Tampa a direct hit.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's small-dollar donor fundraising is beset by confusion and fatigue
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump’s contributions from small-dollar donors have plummeted since his last bid for the White House, presenting the former president with a financial challenge as he attempts to keep pace with Democrats' fundraising machine.
item.dc:creator: DAN MERICA, AARON KESSLER and RICHARD LARDNER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A New York village known for its majestic mute swans faces a difficult choice after one is killed
item.description: MANLIUS, N.Y. (AP) — Elegant white swans have an outsize presence in this upstate New York village measuring less than 2 square miles. Their likeness is on village flags, community centers and welcome signs. “Swan Fest” is celebrated each fall.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video: Hurricane Milton sucks water out of Tampa Bay
item.description: Tampa Bay experienced a rare reverse storm surge as Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: 20 lbs. of cocaine found in truck floorboard at South Texas port of entry
item.description: A truck driven by a U.S. citizen had over 20 pounds of cocaine hidden in the floorboards as it tried to enter the South Texas border from Mexico, U.S. Customs and Border Protection officials said.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Watch: Massive 28-foot wave captured inside Hurricane Milton
item.description: Drone footage captured a monstrous wave inside Hurricane Milton as the powerful storm passed through the Gulf of Mexico on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KREX 5

item.title: Polluted waste from Florida's fertilizer industry is in the path of Milton's fury
item.description: As Hurricane Milton pummeled Florida’s west coast with powerful winds and flooding rain, environmentalists worry it could scatter the polluted leftovers of the state’s phosphate fertilizer mining industry and other hazardous waste across the peninsula and into vulnerable waterways.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BIESECKER and JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Milton makes landfall, slamming into Florida with destructive winds, catastrophic storm surge
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday night, with experts predicting it could be the worst storm in the area in decades. Millions of people had their power knocked out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: COVID may increase risk of heart attack, death years after infection: study
item.description: COVID-19 could increase the risk of major cardiac events, like strokes and heart attacks, or death from any cause, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Brown rejects calls to divest from companies in connection with pro-Palestinian protests on campus
item.description: Brown University has rejected a proposal to divest from 10 companies that protesters say were facilitating the Israeli occupation of Palestinian territory.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The best Amazon October Prime Day competitor sales
item.description: Walmart, Target and Wayfair have announced absolutely massive, site-wide sales with deep discounts on all kinds of items.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, NewsNation

item.title: Levi's are deeply discounted for October Prime Day, with jeans starting at $13
item.description: Right now, you can get classic denim pieces from Levi's for the whole family — at great prices — thanks to October Prime Day.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Thousands of copies of former President Trump's "God Bless the USA" Bible were printed in China, according to trade records.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump campaigns in 'make or break' Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Trump barnstormed Pennsylvania on Wednesday as he and his campaign have made clear the commonwealth could make or break his chances of winning in November. Trump held rallies in President Biden’s hometown of Scranton and in Reading.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa's 'Lt. Dan' says he's riding out Hurricane Milton on boat despite mayor's warning
item.description: A Tampa man who goes by the nickname "Lt. Dan" has caused a flurry of online concern for his safety during Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In remote mountain communities cut off by Helene, residents look to the skies for aid
item.description: RAMSEYTOWN, N.C. (AP) — As the Black Hawk helicopter slowly descends in Ramseytown, North Carolina, a plume of sand kicks up. When the dust settles, the sprawling sea of stones and twisted metal beams becomes clear.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nazi-looted Monet, missing for over 80 years, returned to heirs of original owners in New Orleans: FBI
item.description: A Monet artwork believed to have been lost during Nazi occupation in WWII has been returned to its rightful owners after a lengthy investigation spanning decades and continents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jenna Fischer of 'The Office' reveals breast cancer diagnosis, recovery
item.description: Jenna Fischer, the actress well-known for her role as Pam on "The Office," revealed her diagnosis and recovery in a post to social media.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: North Carolina lawmakers pass $273M Helene relief bill with voting changes to more counties
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina legislators completed an initial $273 million relief package on Wednesday to help spur recovery from Hurricane Helene, describing it as a down payment on aid and a way to help hard-hit counties gain more flexibility in holding elections already underway.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Head of international shipping regulator says industry must do more to cut carbon pollution
item.description: HAMBURG, Germany (AP) — For years, the international shipping industry has been criticized for making little progress in reducing the carbon-belching pollution released from the fuels that vessels use in moving most of the cargo that people use every day, such as food, cars and clothing.
item.dc:creator: PETER PRENGAMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LOOK: Tornado crosses Florida interstate as Hurricane Milton churns closer
item.description: As Florida's Gulf Coast braces for the impacts of Hurricane Milton Wednesday, the storm is causing severe weather across the state.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Milton slams Florida as a Category 3 storm; leaving millions in the dark
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday along Florida’s Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm, bringing powerful winds, deadly storm surge and potential flooding to much of the state. Milton drew fuel from exceedingly warm Gulf of Mexico waters, twice reaching Category 5 status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hurricane Milton: Protect your home from looting with these 4 steps
item.description: Emergency preparedness expert Geoff Fahringer explains how to make your home unattractive to would-be looters as Hurricane Milton approaches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton takes aim near Tampa Bay, Florida, once again a Category 5 storm
item.description: Hurricane Milton remains a ferocious storm that could land a once-in-a-century direct hit on Tampa and St. Petersburg, engulfing the populous region with towering storm surges and turning debris from Helene’s devastation 12 days ago into projectiles. While the storm had previously weakened, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Plane evacuating people from Hurricane Milton crashes after takeoff
item.description: A plane carrying passengers evacuating from Hurricane Milton crashed near the southeast corner of the St. Pete Pier Tuesday morning, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: This Montana Senate candidate said his opponent ate 'lobbyist steak.' But he lobbied—with steak
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — During an October debate, Montana GOP Senate candidate Tim Sheehy denounced his Democratic opponent, Sen. Jon Tester, for ” eating lobbyist steak ″ while the Republican fought in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service. How it works
item.description: With Hurricane Milton barreling toward Florida's Gulf Coast, many Tampa Bay area residents will be faced with widespread and dayslong power outages, which could affect cellphone service, as hundreds saw during Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: U.S. Postal Service announces recommended 2024 holiday shipping dates
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service announced its recommended mailing and shipping dates on Tuesday for the 2024 holiday season. The following list shows the latest recommended dates for a package sent to an address in the contiguous United States to be delivered by Dec.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Melania Trump waves off 'hand-swatting incident': A 'mere misunderstanding'
item.description: Melania Trump is waving off speculation that she didn't want to hold her husband's hand, chalking up images of her seemingly "swatting" away former President Donald Trump to a "mere misunderstanding.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform is addictive, harms kids' mental health
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia sued TikTok on Tuesday, alleging the platform exploits and harms young users while "deceiving" the public about these dangers.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Canyoneer dies after accidental 150-foot fall at Zion National Park
item.description: A man fell while canyoneering at Zion National Park on Saturday, resulting in his death, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, WGN 9

item.title: FEMA administrator continues pushback against false claims as Helene death toll hits 230
item.description: LAKE LURE, N.C. (AP) — The head of the U.S. disaster response agency continued to forcefully push back Monday against false claims and conspiracy theories about her agency’s response to Hurricane Helene as the death toll from the storm continued to climb.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY and GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Are hurricanes intensifying more rapidly than ever before?
item.description: Rapid intensification is defined by the National Hurricane Center as an increase of at least 30 knots (about 35 mph) in maximum sustained winds within a 24-hour period, which can mean a jump of two categories on the Saffir-Simpson scale.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC
